stories.title: Iran reportedly closes Strait of Hormuz again as Vance heads to Switzerland for talks
stories.summary: Iran's joint military command said the closure was in response to continued Israeli military operations in Lebanon.

stories.title: Iran says it closed Strait of Hormuz after Israel and Hizbollah exchange fire
stories.summary: Set-back comes as US vice-president heads to Switzerland for peace talks with negotiators from the Islamic republic

stories.title: US-Iran Talks Set to Open With Vance Due at Swiss Venue
stories.summary: US-Iran talks on a permanent ceasefire were set to kick off Sunday with top officials including US Vice President JD Vance converging in Switzerland, even after Iran issued a renewed order to close the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Fed’s Favorite Gauge Is Seen Showing Faster Inflation
stories.summary: The latest update to the Federal Reserve’s favorite inflation gauge is unlikely to challenge a growing consensus at the US central bank around the need for interest-rate hikes this year.

stories.title: Cross-border biotech deals grow more complex as US targets China investment links
stories.summary: Cross-border biotech deals between China and the United States are becoming more complicated and could face a modest slowdown as Washington steps up efforts to restrict investment and technology transfers, according to industry analysts. “There will be more geopolitical scrutiny going forward. This scrutiny may lead to slightly fewer deals than without it,” said Diederik Stadig, senior healthcare

stories.title: A Stock Trader’s Guide to Navigating a Rare ‘Super El Niño’
stories.summary: As concerns over the Iran war recede, stock investors are confronting another threat: climate risk, which is prompting a reassessment of bets across sectors from agriculture to insurance.

stories.title: Iran Sanctions Losing Efficacy
stories.summary: In 2015, sanctions played a key role in bringing Iran to the negotiating table but now warfare has been the primary tool. Oliver Wyman Partner and Global Anti-Financial Crime Practice Leader Daniel Tannebaum explains to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend how even if sanctions on Iran are lifted, businesses may remain hesitant to reengage with Iran due to lingering risks. (S

stories.title: Bitcoin's future as revolutionary as the smartphone, according to CoinDesk
stories.summary: CoinDesk’s president of indices and data has a message for investors: Don’t count out bitcoin.

stories.title: What to Watch at London Climate Week
stories.summary: The city is hosting CEOs, policymakers and activists to discuss some of the most pressing climate issues.

stories.title: Rahm Emanuel says Trump ‘got schooled’ by Iran in bad ceasefire deal
stories.summary: Possible Democratic presidential candidate describes war as historic ‘American national security mess’

stories.title: Brazil’s Lula Holds Lead Against Bolsonaro After Bank Scandal
stories.summary: President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva maintained his lead in polls on the Brazilian presidential election against Senator Flavio Bolsonaro, who is weighed down by his ties to a former banker involved in the country’s biggest bank fraud scandal.

stories.title: Rep. Warnock: Wall Street vs Main Street
stories.summary: Senator Raphael Warnock (D-GA) tells David Gura on Bloomberg This Weekend that the focus of the country should be on how local Main Streets are doing, not on well Wall Street is doing. Watch part one of Senator Warnock's interview now and tune in to Bloomberg This Weekend tomorrow for part two. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Iran Says Hormuz Closed Again as Talks With US Set to Open
stories.summary: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz for shipping transit due to what it called Israel’s violation of a ceasefire, even as Pakistan said US-Iran talks were set to open in Switzerland on Sunday.

stories.title: US-Iran Delegations Expected in Switzerland
stories.summary: Three days after President Trump signed an agreement with Iran, Switzerland is playing host for diplomatic talks with the United States, Iran, Pakistan, and Qatar in attendance. The Associated Press's International Correspondent Philip Crowther joins Bloomberg This Weekend with hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini to discuss who may be attending this summit and what a recent strikes in Lebanon c

stories.title: Iraq Tells Oil Fields to Start Lifting Output After US-Iran Deal
stories.summary: Iraq asked operators of five major oil fields to boost production to prewar levels, targeting output of over 3 million barrels a day, after US-Iran deal that aims to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: AI buildout gives tech investors new reasons to watch bond market
stories.summary: Tech giants are depleting cash reserves and raising debt in their ambitious data center buildouts, a dynamic that's forcing investors to watch interest rates.

stories.title: Did Anthropic talk its way into an AI export ban?
stories.summary: FT analysis shows company warned about dangers of advanced AI far more than rival OpenAI this year

stories.title: Pakistani Premier Heading to Switzerland to Join US-Iran Talks
stories.summary: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and military chief Asim Munir are heading to Switzerland to take part in US-Iran talks, the government announced Saturday, bringing one of the key mediators between the two adversaries to the table.

stories.title: Millions in Brazil Get Fake Government Mobile Alert After Hack
stories.summary: Brazil’s Integration and Regional Development Ministry and federal police are investigating an intrusion into the civil defense alert system that sent messages containing the word “misanthropy” to the mobile phones of millions of Brazilians.

stories.title: Trump’s Fears About Economy Undercut US Leverage in Iran Talks
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the prospect of global economic collapse was a big reason he signed an interim peace deal with Iran. That admission exposes a key US weakness heading into the next round of talks with Tehran.

stories.title: Iran Resumes Kharg Island Oil Loadings After US Blockade Lifted
stories.summary: Iran has resumed loading crude from its Kharg island export terminal after a break of about six weeks, following the lifting of a US Navy blockade of its ports.

stories.title: Netanyahu Faces MOU Blow
stories.summary: While the United States and Iran have a new Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), Israel has been sidelined from the talks. Bloomberg News' Jerusalem reporter Dan Williams explains the embarrassing situation for Prime Minister Netanyahu to David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Warns Inflation Expectations Are Rising
stories.summary: South Africa’s central bank governor said policymakers are seeing early signs of second-round inflation effects as underlying price pressures build, emphasizing the need to act.

stories.title: The Reindustrialize Summit: 'Build, Baby, Build'
stories.summary: At the Reindustrialize Summit in Detroit, industrial leaders, White House officials, and major investors convened to emphasize the critical role of manufacturing in reinforcing national and military strength. Axios Defense reporter Colin Demarest joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the messaging at the summit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Iran-US Talks to Take Place Sunday in Switzerland, Pakistan Says
stories.summary: Iran and the US will hold technical-level talks on Sunday in Switzerland, Pakistan’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs said.

stories.title: MOU Gives US and Iran Breathing Room
stories.summary: Georgetown University School of Foreign Service Adjunct Professor Ali Vaez joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend and explains his cautious optimism for the recent Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) involving the US, Israel, and Iran. While Vaez acknowledges a deal within the 60 day timeframe is unlikely, the MOU allows for that timeframe to be extended and should be viewe

stories.title: Driving an EV in These US States Saves You the Most Money
stories.summary: Some of the places with the most expensive gas have the cheapest electricity, including Arizona, Utah and Washington.

stories.title: Vance Says He Expects to Leave in Couple of Days for Iran Talks
stories.summary: “I expect that I will leave sometime the next couple of days” to join US negotiators Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff in Switzerland for talks with Iran, Vice President JD Vance says in an interview with Fox News.

stories.title: Trump Seeks Iran Offramp
stories.summary: President and Founder of Eurasia Group and GZERO Media Ian Bremmer joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss how in the face of the United States failing to achieve any of the war goals given at the beginning of the conflict, President Trump is looking to put Iran behind him. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: US-Iran Nuclear Talks Stall as Fighting Rages On in Lebanon
stories.summary: Fighting between Israel and Hezbollah continued unabated in southern Lebanon, casting fresh uncertainty over planned talks between the US and Iran to permanently end the war in the Middle East and curb Tehran’s nuclear program.

stories.title: SpaceX IPO Sparks Anticipation for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.summary: The capital influx from the SpaceX IPO, which ended its first week of trading up 37% from its IPO price, highlights strong investor enthusiasm. Bloomberg Radio host of "Masters in Business" Barry Ritholtz comes on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss the potential impact of this enthusiasm on the upcoming IPOs of OpenAI and Anthropic, expected to go public in the near future. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Ukraine Targets Oil Refinery 2,000 Kilometers Inside Russia
stories.summary: Ukraine targeted the Tyumen oil refinery in Russia’s Ural region, about 2,000 kilometers (1,240 miles) from the Ukrainian border, extending Kyiv’s campaign against Russia’s energy infrastructure.

stories.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
stories.summary: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

stories.title: Jio Platforms Plans $3 Billion Debt Reduction from IPO Proceeds
stories.summary: Jio Platforms Ltd. plans to use part of the proceeds from its proposed initial share sale to repay its telecom unit’s external commercial borrowings of almost $3 billion, a draft IPO document showed.

stories.title: Massive bonuses for South Korea's chip workers puts central bank on inflation alert
stories.summary: Workers from tech industries receive bonus worth millions of won, prompting the Bank of Korea to warn of the upward pressure to inflation.

stories.title: Odd Lots: How Substack Creators Are Thinking About AI (Podcast)
stories.summary: We closed out our New York live show on May 28 with a panel that featured three of our favorite Substackers: James van Geelen of Citrini Research, Sam Ro, founder of The TKer, and journalist Jasmine Sun. They’ve all been Odd Lots guests before, and we wanted to get them together to discuss how journalists and analysts are supposed to cover this incredibly strange and highly pressurized moment in m

stories.title: BYD Rejects Claims It Violated Hungary’s Environmental Rules
stories.summary: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD Co. said it rejected allegations it breached environmental rules in Hungary during the construction of its first plant in the European Union.

stories.title: Europe’s Stocks Are Back in the Lead as Stagflation Risks Ease
stories.summary: The prospect of peace in the Middle East is setting up European stocks for a standout second half, as investors bet on stronger economic growth and easing inflation.

stories.title: Germany’s Weirdest Debt Market May No Longer Be Safe Haven for Lenders
stories.summary: After storied Austrian motorcycle manufacturer KTM AG presented an insolvency plan to creditors last year, several of them convened a video conference to discuss a counter-proposal. More than 100 parties joined, but none of the usual big corporate lenders were involved. Instead, the group featured a motley crew of backers from tiny German towns, Chinese banks and European pension funds, all with w

stories.title: Europe’s stocks offer a peace dividend their US peers can’t match
stories.summary: European companies have more to gain by way of recovery if energy shortages caused by the Iran conflict ease

stories.title: The window for peace in Ukraine won’t be open forever
stories.summary: There is an opportunity to freeze the conflict, but Putin’s fantasy of total victory could get in the way

stories.title: Trump Tells Axios He Doesn’t See Anthropic as US Security Threat
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he doesn’t view Anthropic PBC as a national-security threat, days after his administration took steps to cut off foreign access to the tech company’s most advanced artificial intelligence models.

stories.title: Nobel Winner John Jumper to Leave Google DeepMind for Anthropic
stories.summary: Google DeepMind Vice President John Jumper, who won the 2024 Nobel Prize in chemistry for his work on artificial intelligence, is leaving the company to join Anthropic PBC.

stories.title: This year, the Oscars of the food world wasn’t really about food
stories.summary: At Monday’s James Beard Awards in Chicago, winner after winner stood up for the immigrants who keep America’s restaurants running

stories.title: Fertiliser prices tumble as traders look beyond Middle East disruption
stories.summary: Urea back to prewar levels but drop in demand ‘is not good news’

stories.title: Bob Iger’s long goodbye
stories.summary: For two decades, Hollywood’s most indispensable executive has been synonymous with Disney. Can he really tear himself away?

stories.title: From Alo to Texas Chicken, foreign brands bet on China despite stiff rivalry
stories.summary: Premium and niche foreign brands are entering or returning to China as they seek to unlock growth in the country’s vast consumer market just as many international companies are either pulling out or closing shop amid fierce competition in a fast-changing environment. Foreign companies including American firms Alo Yoga and Church’s Texas Chicken (operating as Texas Chicken internationally), German

stories.title: Is Bitcoin-Backed Digital Credit Dead After MicroStrategy’s STRC Crash?
Third Heathrow runway would add just 0.05% to UK GDP, government finds
stories.summary: Forecast by Department for Transport is a tenth of level predicted by the airport

stories.title: Struggling to find a job? Try looking in Nevada
stories.summary: The sparsely populated state known for its world-class casinos and dry climate has been a hiring hot spot.

stories.title: Grifols (GRFS) Launches FESILTY in the U.S. for Congenital Fibrinogen Deficiency Treatment
'The Stringer' defies critics with four Emmy documentary nominations
Deadly H5 Bird Flu Spreads Worldwide With Australia Outbreak
stories.summary: Australia reported its first mainland case of H5 bird flu, marking the spread of the deadly virus to every continent on the planet.

Trump accepts Qatar jet as new Air Force One despite ethics concerns
stories.summary: The $400mn gift has raised charges of conflicts between personal interests and official duties

stories.title: Value Investing Legend Seth Klarman: Masters in Business
stories.summary: Barry sits down with Munger disciple Seth Klarman, CEO of Baupost Group, a Boston-based investment manager with a multi-strategy approach. They discuss Seth's start as a 25 year-old and journey to CEO. They also discuss his approach to risk, IPOs, and sectors along with his sports passions including a smaller ownership in the Boston Red Sox, horse racing, and his feelings about the Boston Celtics'

stories.title: Trump Displays Refitted Qatar-Gifted Jet to Be Air Force One
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Friday unveiled the Boeing Co. 747-8 that will serve as the new US presidential jet, proclaiming the gifted plane from the Qatari government “virtually double the size” of the previous model.

stories.title: Buy These 3 Growth Stocks Now, Ignore the Noise, and Thank Yourself Later
AppLovin Could Hit $600+ With 28% Upside as AI Ad-Tech Story Rebounds
Judge Says DOJ Can Hand Biden Recordings to Heritage Foundation
stories.summary: A federal judge in Washington denied former President Joe Biden’s request to block the Justice Department from turning over tapes and transcripts of interviews he privately recorded almost a decade ago to a conservative advocacy group, but will temporarily bar the release while he appeals.

stories.title: Germany’s financial watchdog removes three Berenberg bosses
stories.summary: BaFin’s move comes as country’s oldest lender reports possible breaches of corporate governance

stories.title: Fedspeak vs. war deal: Here are the things that drove this week's stock market
stories.summary: The S&P 500 surged on Thursday, making up for losses seen after central bankers convened.

UnitedHealth’s $3 Billion AI Push Has Bots Calling Doctors
stories.summary: At UnitedHealth Group Inc., artificial intelligence reads aloud summaries of medical charts as nurses drive to patients’ homes. It listens to millions of customer calls to find the causes of complaints. One trial even has AI agents calling doctors’ offices to schedule appointments for patients.

stories.title: Signal’s Whittaker on Big Tech’s Privacy Threat
stories.summary: "Three companies... can make decisions that fundamentally harm our collective cybersecurity." Signal President Meredith Whittaker tells Mishal Husain why she believes AI and big tech pose a major threat to privacy. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Spain Weighs Financial Aid to Struggling Solar Power Industry
stories.summary: The Spanish government is weighing whether to offer financial support to a domestic solar industry that’s grappling with oversupply and negative wholesale power prices.

stories.title: 29-year-old making $250k in tech went 'undercover' at a coffee chain before opening her own matcha cafe
stories.summary: Michelle Yeung wasn't happy in tech, despite earning $250,000 a year. Before opening Matcha House, she spent months learning the business.
